title: ServiceContract and OperationContract attributes should be preferred together
description: "ServiceContract" and "OperationContract" attributes should be used togetherAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellapi-designWhy is this an issue?TheServiceContractattribute specifies that a class or interface defines the communication contract of a Windows Communication Foundation (WCF) service. The service operations of this class or interface are defined byOperationContractattributes added to methods. It doesn’t make sense to define a contract without any service operations; thus, in aServiceContractclass or interface at least one method should be annotated withOperationContract.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

triggering_conditions.description: ServiceContract attribute present AND no OperationContract attributes in the same class/interface
triggering_conditions.logic: Custom function handles correlation between attributes and type declarations

exclusion_conditions.description: Comments, logging, string formatting, unit tests, and other non-functional contexts
exclusion_conditions.handled_by: Custom function with built-in exclusion logic

logic.reasoning: Uses custom function to correlate ServiceContract attributes with their target classes/interfaces, then checks if any OperationContract attributes exist within the type body. This approach handles the attribute-to-type relationship that simple regex cannot capture. Built-in exclusions prevent false positives in logging, comments, and test code.

should_not_trigger: public class RegularClass { public void Method() {} }
